penalty_extra_disagreement.analysis: Same rubric-scope issue seen on V20/V22. GPT-4o's rubric-strict read is correct per the uploaded prompt.

fls_score_reconciliation.reconciliation_note: Both teachers independently identify V26 as the SERIES BEST to date. Near-exact agreement on total time (Δ0.9s), knot sequence, drain closure, and rubric compliance. FLS delta of 3.9 is entirely from Claude's out-of-rubric quality penalties. Rubric-strict reconciled value is 503.9 (GPT-4o). V26 is now the first V-series video to break the 500-point ceiling under any scoring.

systematic_findings.new_series_ceiling: V26 is the first V-series attempt to break 500 FLS. Both teachers agree. This confirms the trainee's true capability is at the ~500 tier when fresh and using familiar equipment.
systematic_findings.drain_agreement_continues: V26 is the 6th drain-closure agreement case (V12, V13, V18, V19, V22, V26). Running tally: AGREE 6, DIVERGE 5 (V11, V14, V16, V17, V20).
systematic_findings.rubric_scope_issue_recurring: Third video (V20, V22, V26) where Claude applies an out-of-rubric quality penalty. Strongly recommends formalizing the 'placement + gap + knot + avulsion + hand switch' penalty set in the prompt and adding a separate cosmetic_quality field if we want to track other signals.
systematic_findings.economy_ceiling_identified: Both teachers identify the third_throw as the biggest remaining time sink even on the series-best run. GPT-4o's phase split: placement 8.7s, throws 75.3s, cut 4.5s. The trainee's cut phase on V26 is ~4.5s — the fastest ever — showing the long-tail V20 issue has been solved. The remaining lever is knot economy during throws 1 and 3.
